revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ajihayao-jinja]][[ja:阿遅速雄神社]] {{Expand Japanese|阿遅速雄神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Achihayao Shrine | native_name = 阿遅速雄神社 | image = Ajihayao-jinja01.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Achihayao Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.688766|135.565312}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|es|Ajisukitakahikone|fr|Ajisukitakahikone|hu|Adzsiszukitakahikone|it|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|mni|ꯑꯖꯤꯁꯨꯀꯤꯇꯀꯍꯤꯀꯣꯅꯦ|ru|Адзи-суки-така-хико-нэ|simple|Ajisukitakahikone|zh|味耜高彥根神|qid=Q506610|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} }} {{nihongo|'''Achihayao Shrine'''|阿遅速雄神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province|ja|摂津国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 18 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039202|ltq=Q135039202|qid=Q135039202|1=Himekosono Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Ikasuri Shrine|lt=Ikasuri Shrine|en|Ikasuri Shrine|fr|Ikasuri-jinja|ja|坐摩神社|simple|Ikasuri Shrine|zh|坐摩神社|qid=Q11425667|1=Ikasuri Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q35765|ltq=Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de Settsu|hak|Settsu-koet|id|Provinsi Settsu|it|Settsu (provincia)|ja|摂津国|ko|셋쓰국|lt|Seccu žemė|nl|Settsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Settsu|ru|Сэтцу (провинция)|simple|Settsu Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Q10870274|ltq=Q10870274|qid=Q10870274|1=Higashinari district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.688766|135.565312}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Ajihayao-jinja01.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|es|Ajisukitakahikone|fr|Ajisukitakahikone|hu|Adzsiszukitakahikone|it|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|mni|ꯑꯖꯤꯁꯨꯀꯤꯇꯀꯍꯤꯀꯣꯅꯦ|ru|Адзи-суки-така-хико-нэ|simple|Ajisukitakahikone|zh|味耜高彥根神|qid=Q506610|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181038 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4120005001795 == Commons category (P373) == * Ajihayao-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府大阪市鶴見区放出東3-31-18', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ajihayao-jinja}} [[JA:阿遅速雄神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11657712}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ajihayao Shrine |Image = [[File:Ajihayao-jinja01.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Tsurumi Ward (Osaka City)|Q490526|qid=Q490526|lt=Tsurumi Ward|1=Tsurumi-ku, Osaka}} Hanaten-higashi 3-31-18 |Coordinates = {{Coordinate template degrees minutes seconds|34|41|19|N|135|33|53|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}; formerly {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}} |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate names = Kusanagi-no-miya, Yatsurugi Shrine |Annual festival = October 20–21 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Ajihayao
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ajihayao Shrine''' (あちはやをじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Ward (Osaka City)|Q490526|qid=Q490526|lt=Tsurumi Ward|1=Tsurumi-ku, Osaka}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Yatsurugi Shrine, but {{ill|lt=Namikawa Seisho|Q6563872|qid=Q6563872|1=Namikawa Seisho}}, who identified shrines listed in the Engishiki, determined that this shrine corresponded to the Ajihayao Shrine mentioned therein and erected a “stone shrine-name marker of Ajihayao Shrine.” From that time on, it came to be known as Ajihayao Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * The principal deity is {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} (あぢすきたかひこね). * Also enshrined is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Murakumo-no-Tsurugi|Q14944|qid=Q14944|lt=Yatsurugi Ōkami|1=Kusanagi no Tsurugi}} (やつるぎだいじん). === History === According to shrine tradition, the {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} {{ill|lt=Ajisukitakahikone|Q506610|qid=Q506610|lt=Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami|1=Ajisukitakahikone}} descended to {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, opened up the land, and taught the people agriculture. He was worshipped as the guardian deity of this area. The origin of the shrine is said to date to {{ill|lt=668|Q30116|qid=Q30116|1=668}} (the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), during the {{ill|lt=Kusanagi Sword theft incident|Q861812|qid=Q861812|1=theft of Kusanagi no Tsurugi}}. When the monk Dōgyō of {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}} attempted to flee back to {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}} by boat with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi Sword|1=UNKNOWN}}, he was caught in a sudden {{ill|lt=storm|Q81054|qid=Q81054|1=Storm}}. Fearing divine punishment, he threw the sword into a river mouth along the way. The sword was later recovered by local villagers and temporarily enshrined at this shrine. It is said that the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi Sword|1=UNKNOWN}} was subsequently safely returned to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. During the Meiji period, the shrine was relocated to its present site due to the construction of the {{ill|lt=Naniwa Railway|Q11558476|qid=Q11558476|1=Naniwa Railway}} (now the {{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|qid=Q1073379|1=Katamachi Line}}). In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 ({{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}}), at the initiative of representatives of the {{ill|lt=parishioners|Q125562175|qid=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}}, a {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was built. At the same time, major improvements were made to the shrine grounds, including repairs to the main hall, expansion of the sacred precincts, and construction of subsidiary shrines. On the grounds remains a “Kikōhi” commemorative monument inscribed by {{ill|lt=Governor of Osaka Prefecture|Q15063280|qid=Q15063280|1=Governor of Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Satō Yoshinori|Q11479713|qid=Q11479713|1=Gisen Satō}}. According to the inscription on the Kikōhi monument in the shrine grounds Even today, on the day of the {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}, the chief priest or shrine staff from {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} pay a visit, and on the festival day of {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}}, the priests of this shrine attend there, a custom that continues. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Kinshi reishikeidaikinban.jpg|thumb|140px|left|The [[golden kite–shaped large brocade banner|Kinshi Reishikei Daikinban]] displayed only on special occasions, based on a myth that forms the origin of {{ill|lt=Hanaten|Q11498911|qid=Q11498911|1=Hanaten}}]] ;Shrine buildings :The main hall is built in the nagare-zukuri style with copper roofing. ;Shrine office ;Main gate :Tiled roof. It is also called the Satsuma Gate, based on a tale that it was relocated from a nearby Satsuma residence in the early Meiji period. ;Stone shrine-name marker of Ajihayao Shrine :A granite marker standing in front of the main hall. It is a square pillar 96 cm high and 24 cm on each side, engraved with “Ajihayao Shrine.” It was erected between {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|qid=Q6989|1=1736}} and {{ill|lt=1737|Q6993|qid=Q6993|1=1737}} ({{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} 1–2) by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kubo Shigeyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}}, a disciple of Namikawa Seisho, who aimed to honor Shikinaisha in the Kinai region. The marker stands on a lower base stone 43 cm high and 61 cm square, and a middle base stone 24 cm high and 42 cm square. The middle base bears the name “Sugahirofusa,” referring to Yamaguchiya Iheei, who funded its construction. In {{ill|lt=2004|Q2014|qid=Q2014|1=2004}}, it was designated a {{ill|lt=Tangible Cultural Property|Q2901860|qid=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Osaka City. {{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000117783.html |title=Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties (FY2010) – Okage Lantern, 1 item (Ajihayao Shrine) |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2014-02-15}} ;Okage Lantern :A lantern made in {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Keiō|Q776775|qid=Q776775|1=Keiō}} 4), standing beside the main gate. It was erected as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=bond of pilgrimage|1=UNKNOWN}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okage pilgrimage|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the only surviving Okage lantern in Osaka City. In {{ill|lt=2010|Q1995|qid=Q1995|1=2010}}, it was designated an {{ill|lt=Folk Cultural Property|Q24895900|qid=Q24895900|lt=Tangible Folk Cultural Property|1=Folk Cultural Property}} of {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. {{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000012464.html |title=Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties (FY2004) – Shrine-name markers of Amamizu Wake Toyoura-no-Mikoto Shrine, Kamisumuchi Shrine, Tame Shrine, and Ajihayao Shrine |publisher=Osaka City |accessdate=2014-02-15}} ;Great camphor tree :A giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} said to be 1,000 years old, 16 meters tall with a trunk circumference of 6 meters. The main trunk died after being struck by {{ill|lt=lightning|Q15120872|qid=Q15120872|1=Lightning strike}} in the Taishō period, but the subsidiary trunks remain healthy thanks to treatment by {{ill|lt=tree doctors|Q24893891|qid=Q24893891|1=tree doctors}}. On February 20, {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|qid=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} by {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} under the name “Camphor Tree of Ajihayao Shrine.” {{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/atihayaojinja_kusu.html |title=Osaka Prefecture Designated Natural Monument: Camphor Tree of Ajihayao Shrine |publisher=Osaka Prefecture |accessdate=2014-02-15}} ;{{ill|lt=Komainu|Q20155846|qid=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} :Two pairs (four statues) stand in front of the main hall. The pair at the rear was dedicated during the 1,300th anniversary festival held in {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 43). The pair at the front was dedicated in {{ill|lt=1820|Q6973|qid=Q6973|1=1820}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 3). {{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/tsurumi/page/0000001385.html |title=The Great Camphor Tree of Ajihayao Shrine |publisher=Tsurumi Ward, Osaka City |accessdate=2014-02-15}} ;Guidepost ;Iris Sacred Pond :Located on the north side of the shrine grounds. According to tradition, when {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} fell ill, Ajisukitakahikone-no-Kami appeared in a dream and said, “If you offer the {{ill|lt=iris|Q158008|qid=Q158008|lt=shōbu|1=Acorus calamus}} from the pond east of the imperial palace, you will recover.” When this was done, the emperor was healed. During the summer festival, irises from this pond are cut, offered before the deities, and then distributed to parishioners. {{Cite book |Japanese |year=2014 |title=Shōbu-goyomi |publisher=Ajihayao Shrine }} The pond was renovated in July {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}}. ;Auxiliary shrine :Standing beside the main hall, enshrining {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|lt=Toshigami|Q3028663|qid=Q3028663|lt=Ōtoshi Ōkami|1=Toshigami}}, {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi Ōkami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Jinushigami|Q4160872|qid=Q4160872|lt=Ōjinushi Ōkami|1=Landlord deity}}, {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi Ōkami|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman}}, and {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|qid=Q11646488|lt=Kotohira Ōkami|1=Kotohira shrines}}. Based on the inscription on the auxiliary shrine plaque Built in July {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}}. ;Taishōgun Shrine :A subsidiary shrine enshrining the {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|qid=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)}}. Built in July {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}}. ;Camphor Tree Inari Shrine :Built in July {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}}. ;Gokoku Shrine :A subsidiary shrine enshrining those who died for the state. Built in July {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}}. File:Ajihayao-jinja02.JPG | Main hall File:Ajihayao-jinja12.jpg | Shrine office File:Ajihayao-jinja06.JPG | Main gate File:Ajihayao-jinja08.jpg | Stone shrine-name marker of Ajihayao Shrine File:Ajihayao-jinja07.JPG | Okage lantern File:Ajihayao-jinja05.JPG | Great camphor tree File:Ajihayao-jinja11.jpg | Iris Sacred Pond File:Ajihayao-jinja09.jpg | Auxiliary shrine File:Ajihayao-jinja13.jpg | Taishōgun Shrine File:Ajihayao-jinja10.jpg | Camphor Tree Inari Shrine File:Ajihayao-jinja04.JPG | Gokoku Shrine === Major festivals === * Summer Festival Eve – third Saturday of July * Summer Festival – third Sunday of July * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Grand Annual Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} – third Saturday and Sunday of October === Access === 200 meters east from {{ill|lt=Hanaten Station|Q762983|qid=Q762983|1=Hanaten Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|qid=Q1073379|lt=Gakken Toshi Line|1=Katamachi Line}} and JR {{ill|lt=Osaka Higashi Line|Q1067370|qid=Q1067370|1=Osaka Higashi Line}}. Faces {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefectural Route 159 Hirano–Moriguchi Line|Q11441277|qid=Q11441277|1=Osaka Prefectural Road Route 159}}. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === External links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai4shibu/tsurumi-ku/04009achihayaojinja.html Ajihayao Shrine] – Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Commons|Category:Ajihayao-jinja}} {{Shinto navbox}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Achihayao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Achihayao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:大阪市鶴見区の歴史]] [[Category:大阪市鶴見区の建築物]] [[Category:放出]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]]
